---
title: "Data Cleaning With A Spec"
subtitle: "PRAMS-shaped messy data"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
---

# Goal

Practice giving an agent good context, good directions, and clear acceptance criteria for data cleaning.

# Dataset

Use:

```text
data/simulated/prams_messy_for_cleaning.csv
```

Reference codebook:

```text
data/codebooks/simulated_prams_messy_for_cleaning.md
```

# Cleaning Spec

Produce a cleaned dataset with these columns:

- `state`: state name as title case text.
- `subgroup_category`: cleaned subgroup category.
- `subgroup`: cleaned subgroup label.
- `measure`: cleaned measure label.
- `estimate_percent`: numeric percentage estimate.
- `estimate_available`: logical indicator for whether an estimate was available.
- `year`: numeric year.

# Required Recodes

- Convert percent strings like `53.5%` to numeric values like `53.5`.
- Treat `Suppressed`, `NA`, `--`, and `not available` as missing estimates.
- Standardize capitalization and obvious spelling/punctuation inconsistencies.
- Trim leading/trailing whitespace from all character fields.
- Preserve original rows; do not silently drop missing estimates.

# Acceptance Criteria

Your notebook should include:

- A short cleaning plan before code.
- Cleaning code.
- A before/after glimpse of the data.
- A table showing missingness before and after cleaning.
- A brief data dictionary for the cleaned output.
- A list of recoding decisions you verified manually.

# Work Area

```{r}
# Start here.
```
